finding: in-run retry loop re-runs non-idempotent dispatch: a transient-classified workflow failure/timeout re-spawns the whole workflow (duplicate/overlapping execution, 3x cost); a prompt post-generation blip re-runs the turn (double side effects)

an ALLOW-LISTED approval-required tool is not auto-approved in an unattended run — it falls through to tools_needing_approval (orphan pending rows + pause + omitted from skipped report), so the allow-list feature does nothing for ManualApprove users

preemptive_pause_reason false-positives: a first-fire error before create_conversation sets last_status but leaves bound_conversation_id NULL, so the next tick wrongly pauses conversation_deleted and short-circuits transient tolerance

on_change early-return buries skipped-tools: when text is unchanged the fn returns before building the notification, so an unattended firing that skipped tools reports a silent clean success (ITEM-17 honesty guard bypassed)
required typed workflow inputs are not validated: validate() only JSON-checks the fallback branch; a required declared input left blank is submitted as empty string
prompt-task model access not re-validated at fire time (assistant is, model is not) — a user removed from the provider group keeps invoking it via the firing
test-fire ignores the unattended policy (no mcp_config/unattended) so Test attaches ALL accessible servers incl Always-mode pre-exec — more tool access than the real scheduled run
UnattendedToolGrant::list_allows is dead code (live path uses the generic unattended_tool_allowed JSON check)
DOW_SHORT[7] is undefined: isDowList regex accepts digit 7 (valid Sunday in some cron dialects) rendering 'Weekly on undefined'
AllowedToolsField drops tool_name on edit — a per-tool grant is silently widened to whole-server on re-save
notification skipped-tools copy uses emoji + lazy 'tool(s)' pluralization ('1 tool(s) skipped')
day-toggle aria-label uses the abbreviation ('Mon') not the full weekday name

test-fire fidelity/security gap: Test attaches ALL accessible servers (no unattended/mcp_config) so it can pre-execute Always-mode side effects on servers NOT in the task's allow-list — Test is less constrained than the real scheduled run
ITEM-18b resolves get_user_defaults once PER tool-step in a standalone/scheduled run; the user's default disabled-set is run-invariant and could be resolved once per run

the 34 enumerated tests (unattended deny, skipped-tools, preemptive pause, retry, prune, model/allow-list validation, elicit-reject, multi-day, pickers) are added in Phase 8; the retry stop-condition + new branches must be exercised, not asserted trivially

the pre-emptive-pause path logs (warn) and continues on mark_fired/insert_run errors rather than propagating — acceptable for a fire-and-forget sweep but a persistent DB fault would silently re-attempt the pause each tick

BLIND-REVIEW: verified skipped_tools is collected from get_message_with_content(assistant_message_id). Confirmed the denial tool_result blocks (mcp.rs:2296 unattended_denied marker) are appended to context.message_id (the SAME assistant message) across all loop iterations via append_content, so a multi-iteration agentic run does not lose earlier-iteration denials. NOT a defect.

BLIND-REVIEW: unattended prompt run sets mcp_config.mcp_servers = build_unattended_mcp_servers(grants) (allow-listed only) AND unattended=true (deny-not-pause gate) AND re-checks model provider access at fire time. Double-gate is sound: a non-allow-listed side-effecting server neither attaches nor executes. NOT a defect.

BLIND-REVIEW: update_task re-validates assistant ownership, model access, and allow-list accessibility on change, and re-enforces the active-task quota on disabled->enabled transition using count_active_for_user (which excludes disabled rows, so no off-by-one). Matches create-time gating. NOT a defect.

BLIND-REVIEW: preemptive_pause_reason gates conversation_deleted on last_status in {completed,no_change} (a prior successful bind) so a failed-first-fire (bound NULL + last_status='failed') is not mis-paused, and a genuine first run (both NULL) still creates its conversation. Logic is consistent with the stated DEC-6 intent. NOT a defect.

BLIND-REVIEW: FE skippedToolsNote only counts array length; backend SkippedTool is {tool_name,reason} while the e2e fixture (paused-and-runs.spec.ts) uses {server_id,tool_name}. Shape drift in the test fixture vs the real backend schema is a test-fidelity gap, not a runtime bug (the note ignores element fields). A future FE change reading .reason/.tool_name would not be caught by the current mock.

BLIND-REVIEW: FormField injects value/onChange(value)/aria-* via Controller+cloneElement (verified in kit/form.tsx); the forwardRef pickers declare exactly that surface and re-forward aria-describedby/labelledby. Binding + accessible-name wiring is correct. NOT a defect.

BLIND-REVIEW: humanizeCron isDowList (/^\d(,\d)*$/) only accepts single-digit dow fields and normalizes Number%7 so cron dow 7 renders 'Sun' (fixes prior 'undefined'). An out-of-range digit (8,9) would map via %7 to a valid day label, but such values are rejected by backend cron validation before persistence, so unreachable in practice. NOT a defect.

BLIND-REVIEW: migration from useState FormState to react-hook-form; validate/buildBody read form.getValues() at save/test time while hasDeclaredInputs/declaredInputs derive from form.watch('workflow_id') (kept in sync via RHF subscription). Required-input enforcement is done explicitly (FormField required is decorative w/o a resolver) per the in-code note. Consistent. NOT a defect.

BLIND-REVIEW: standalone/scheduled tool-step now honors the user's DEFAULT MCP disabled-servers and fails CLOSED on any get_user_defaults DB error (returns StepResult::Failed), closing the gap where a scheduled workflow ignored the disabled set. Security gate is correct. NOT a defect (perf note re per-step re-resolution already recorded).
page has no upper clamp: GET /runs?page=<huge>&per_page=200 overflows i64 in (page-1)*per_page → panic (debug) / negative OFFSET 500 (release) on crafted input
ORDER BY fired_at DESC has no stable tie-breaker, so two runs with identical fired_at can be duplicated on one page and omitted from another
build_series_seed applies no overall length cap (unlike the single-run RESULT_TEXT_MAX); 100 runs x 280-char previews can reach ~30KB seed message
goToConversation uses window.location.href for in-app nav, forcing a full page reload that tears down SPA/store state; codebase convention is react-router navigate
ListPagination renders a page-size selector but onPageSizeChange ignores the size and always reloads per_page=10, making the size control dead/misleading
seriesChoices emits duplicate value 10 ('Last 10' and 'All loaded') when exactly 10 runs are loaded → duplicate Select option keys
after a sync-reload of a now-pruned page (runs=[] but total>0) the 'No runs yet' branch hides ListPagination, stranding the user on an empty page
SeriesChooser uses a persistent Select (value forced to '') as a one-shot action launcher; the file's established action primitive is Dropdown
run-row expand button sets aria-expanded but has no aria-controls to the detail region and no visible chevron affordance

the total===0 fix left a residual: after prune shrinks total to <= perPage while meta.page>=2, a sync reload of the out-of-range page renders 0 rows with ListPagination hidden (total<=perPage), stranding the user on an empty page
